title: Improving Farm-to-Table Biosecurity Measures Against Salmonella
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     one     of     the     leading     causes     of     foodborne     illness     worldwide  ,   posing     a     major     threat     to     public     health     and     food     security  .   Farm-to-table     biosecurity     is     a     comprehensive     approach     that     integrates     measures     across     production  ,   processing  ,   distribution  ,   and   retail   to     minimize     contamination     risk  .   Strengthening     biosecurity     can     significantly     reduce     the     prevalence     of     Salmonella   in   poultry  ,   livestock  ,   and     produce  ,   thereby     lowering     outbreaks     and     healthcare     costs  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     importance     of     preventive     strategies     such   as   on-farm     hygiene  ,   safe     slaughtering     practices  ,   cold     chain     integrity  ,   and     public     awareness     campaigns  . By   implementing     robust     biosecurity     protocols     and     supporting     cross-sector     collaboration  ,   governments     and     stakeholders     can     protect     consumers  ,   maintain     market     confidence  ,   and     ensure     safer     food     supplies     both     domestically     and     internationally  .
